mapping.a_element: fluctuations in speed and direction of motion with independent characteristic time-scales
mapping.b_element: transition from directed propulsion (short times) to random walk (longer times) with a time-scale separation

mapping.a_element: persistent and directed random walks as angular dynamics
mapping.b_element: directed motion at short times and random walk at longer times

mapping.a_element: additive corrections to the diffusion coefficient
mapping.b_element: substantially enhanced diffusion coefficient in the random walk regime

mapping.a_element: velocity autocorrelation as a sum of exponential forms
mapping.b_element: time-dependent transition from directed to diffusive motion

brief_justification: Both papers describe a transition between directed/persistent motion and random/diffusive motion driven by independent time-scales, with the diffusion coefficient modified by the active dynamics. The underlying mechanism is a time-scale-separated stochastic process governing the crossover from ballistic to diffusive regimes.
